To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar variable and $I$ is the identity matrix.

The given matrix is:
$$ A = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} \frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} \end{array}\right) $$

The characteristic polynomial is given by $p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.
$$ A - \lambda I = \left(\begin{array}{ccc} \frac{189}{16} - \lambda & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\ \frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} - \lambda & -\frac{41}{16} \\ \frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} - \lambda \end{array}\right) $$

To simplify the calculation, let's find a common denominator for all entries. The denominators are 16, 4, and 8. The least common multiple is 16.
We can rewrite the matrix $A$ by factoring out $\frac{1}{16}$:
$$ A = \frac{1}{16} \left(\begin{array}{ccc} 189 & -108 & 166 \\ 78 & -86 & -41 \\ 21 & 213 & 70 \end{array}\right) $$
Let $B = 16A$. Then $A = \frac{1}{16}B$.
